summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic Marquee component core for the ars-ui repository (issue #277), covering state, events, context, messages, attrs, and the connect API, then shepherd it through the automated Codex review process.
summary.outcome: PR #705 was opened against fogodev/ars-ui and three Codex review findings were fully resolved: finite loop replay cap enforcement, multi-cause hover/focus pause tracking, and a SyncProps/on_props_changed path for context-backed props. A follow-up commit was pushed with spec, proptest invariant, and machine logic all updated.

repo: PRs that add insta snapshots require the snapshot-reviewed label before Codex will approve them — applied manually before triggering @codex review.
The review loop is driven by GitHub reactions: Codex signals active review with 👀 and approval by adding 👍; unresolved inline threads must be addressed before 👍 appears.
Prop-sync across context and attrs uses a dedicated SyncProps event and on_props_changed handler — the same pattern is used in number_input and popover, making it a repo-wide convention.
cargo xtask spec validate enforces that spec markdown and compiled code snippets stay in sync; spec changes need a matching cargo xtask spec compile-snippets run.
The full pre-push gate (unit, spec_conformance, proptest, insta, clippy, xtask spec validate, xci) runs as a hook before the commit is finalised.

code.finding: State/event handling section; finite loop replay guard and multi-cause pause tracking were both missing here before the review fix.
SyncProps event dispatch and on_props_changed implementation added here to sync disabled/motion fields from Service::set_props into context and attrs.
Test harness uses an exhaustive match on events; adding SyncProps to the production event enum immediately caused a compile error here requiring a new match arm.
Proptest invariant updated to allow hover and focus pause causes to coexist (both may be set simultaneously) while still requiring both to be clear in the Playing state.

Spec described pre-review (buggy) transition behavior for loop exhaustion replay and overlapping pause causes; updated to make prop synchronization, exhausted-loop replay cap, and multi-cause pause part of the formal contract.

workflow: Write regression tests first and confirm they are red before patching machine logic — ensures the fix addresses exactly the reported behavior and not something adjacent.

When adding a new event variant to an exhaustive enum, anticipate compile failures in test harness match arms and fix the exhaustiveness issue as a separate step before observing behavioral test failures.
Run cargo fmt --check as the final step in the verification suite, not the first — formatting drift is a clean-up concern and running it early adds noise before correctness is established.

Poll external review systems (Codex, CI) with generous sleep intervals (60–120s) rather than tight loops to avoid hammering endpoints; track both reactions and unresolved threads separately since a dropped 👀 with zero threads means something different than a dropped 👀 with open threads.
Before patching a component's prop-sync logic, read at least two sibling components' implementations to confirm you're following the established pattern rather than inventing a new one.

friction: Codex review took over 8 minutes of active polling (multiple 60s and 120s sleep intervals) before threads appeared — no webhook or notification mechanism to avoid busy-waiting.
Adding SyncProps to the event enum caused an unexpected compile error in the test harness match arm (lines 988–1030), requiring a separate patch before behavioral test failures could be observed.
cargo fmt --check discovered formatting drift only after the full verification suite had already passed, adding an extra round-trip of fmt + re-check at the end of an otherwise complete fix cycle.
The pre-push hook ran silently for an extended period with no output, making it unclear whether it was still running or had stalled.
open_items: CI matrix jobs (Unit, Release Verification, Adapter, Coverage, feature-flag) were still in progress when the session ended — final green status not confirmed in transcript.
The Codex review thread resolutions and any follow-on 👍 reaction were not confirmed — the session ends after the fix commit is pushed, before Codex re-reviews.
